author: codex-agent

patch.value: Current lifecycle checks rely on static phrase lists. Add configurable pattern policy keys with safe defaults, deterministic diagnostics, and clear source reporting in validate details.
Add settings-backed lifecycle validation pattern controls so closure-like and stale-blocker heuristics can be tuned per project without code changes.

pm validate lifecycle checks read configurable pattern lists from settings with deterministic fallback defaults, expose pattern-source metadata in details, and retain backward-compatible warning tokens unless policy explicitly changes behavior.
Current lifecycle validation baseline is merged and pattern tuning requirements are documented.
Reduce false positives and improve lifecycle validation portability
Projects can tune lifecycle heuristics without patching CLI code
Improves validation signal quality across heterogeneous workflows
Lifecycle validation becomes configurable, deterministic, and easier to adopt
Today’s lifecycle hardening shipped static heuristics; configurable policy prevents future drift/false-positive churn.
Run pm validate --check-lifecycle against projects with different blocker/resolution wording and observe static pattern limitations.
Maintainers get tunable lifecycle diagnostics with less manual suppression.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'validate lifecycle stale blocker configurable patterns' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because existing lifecycle guardrails ship static heuristics but no settings-backed pattern policy surface.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implement deterministic pattern-source reporting (default vs settings override) and preserve warning token stability by default.

message: Create follow-up task for configurable lifecycle validation patterns | explicit_unset=learnings,blocked_by,blocked_reason,unblock_note,resolution,expected_result,actual_result
